texto: As human beings we are plagued with inordinate affections. We love green pieces of paper more than God. We love balls made out of pigskin more than God. We've shown we even love apples more than God. We, like Esau, have traded our birthright- the dignity of our shameless, joy-filled, glory-beholding, glory-reflecting existence- for a bowl of beans.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Matt Papa: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “As human beings we are plagued with inordinate affections. We love green pieces of paper more tha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, god, inspiracional pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e inspiracional.

frase_original: As human beings we are plagued with inordinate affections. We love green pieces of paper more than God. We love balls made out of pigskin more than God. We've shown we even love apples more than God. We, like Esau, have traded our birthright- the dignity of our shameless, joy-filled, glory-beholding, glory-reflecting existence- for a bowl of beans.
